CREATE TABLE test_join_table1 (id Int32, dt64 DateTime64(9));
CREATE TABLE test_join_table2 (ref_id Int32, dt64 DateTime64(9));
SELECT t1.id, toUnixTimestamp64Nano(t1.dt64) AS timestamp1, toUnixTimestamp64Nano(t2.dt64) AS timestamp2 FROM test_join_table1 AS t1 JOIN test_join_table2 AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.ref_id;

WITH toDateTime64('2023-01-01 00:00:00.123456789', 9, 'UTC') AS dt, toDateTime64('1970-01-01 00:00:00.000000000', 9, 'UTC') AS dt2 SELECT toUnixTimestamp64Nano(dt) - toUnixTimestamp64Nano(dt2) AS time_difference;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_datetime64 WHERE toUnixTimestamp64Nano(dt64) > 0;
WITH (SELECT toDateTime64('2023-10-01 12:00:00.987654321', 9, 'UTC')) AS t SELECT toUnixTimestamp64Nano(t) AS utc_timestamp;
WITH toDateTime64('2012-05-01 11:00:00.999999999', 9, 'UTC') AS new_datetime SELECT toUnixTimestamp64Nano(new_datetime) AS timestamp_nano;
